content.verdict: Malliouhana is one of the most architecturally spectacular hotels in the Caribbean — built into a dramatic headland cliff above two of Anguilla's most beautiful beaches, with a famous multi-level cascading pool system that descends the cliff face toward the sea. The Auberge Resorts collection brought new culinary energy and service refinement to this iconic property, while preserving the extraordinary clifftop position and the two private beaches below. For couples who want Caribbean luxury with genuine architectural drama, Malliouhana is in a class of its own on the island.
content.best_room: The Headland Suite is Malliouhana's most dramatic accommodation — perched at the very tip of the cliff headland with panoramic views over both Meads Bay and the Caribbean, an extraordinary multi-terrace private outdoor space, and the feeling of being suspended between sky and sea. The Ocean View Suites along the cliff edge are the best choice for couples who want the full Malliouhana clifftop experience at a more accessible price point.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival on Anguilla
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly into Clayton J. Lloyd International Airport on Anguilla (small island airport with regional connections) or take the 20-minute ferry from St. Martin/Sint Maarten. Transfer to Malliouhana. The hotel's multi-level pool terraces cascading down the cliff toward the sea are visible on arrival — immediately unlike any other Caribbean hotel experience. Sunset cocktails at the clifftop bar.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Meads Bay & Shoal Bay
itinerary_7_nights.description: Anguilla is renowned for its 33 beaches — the finest collection of Caribbean beaches in the Lesser Antilles, with an extraordinary quality of white sand and water clarity. Meads Bay, directly below Malliouhana, is one of the finest; Shoal Bay East on the island's north coast is arguably the most beautiful. The hotel's beach service is impeccable.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cascade Pools & Spa
itinerary_7_nights.description: Malliouhana's multi-level pool system is the most photographed feature of the hotel — three pools at different levels on the cliff face, connected by water features and tropical gardens, with the Caribbean visible below. An entire morning of moving between pool levels is an extraordinary luxury. Afternoon: the Auberge Spa with Caribbean botanical treatments.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Anguilla Island Tour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Anguilla is a small, flat island (26 km long) with an extraordinary density of world-class restaurants for its size and population. A private island tour takes in the limestone cliffs of Little Bay (accessible only by boat or swim), the salt ponds at Sandy Ground with their flamingoes, and a long lunch at Veya or Nota Bene — consistently rated among the Caribbean's finest restaurants.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Snorkeling & Prickly Pear Cays
itinerary_7_nights.description: The offshore Prickly Pear cays are a protected marine area with some of the best snorkeling in Anguilla — shallow clear water over healthy coral with excellent reef fish diversity. A chartered boat excursion to Prickly Pear East cay for a beach picnic and snorkel is a highlight of any Anguilla honeymoon.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sunset Sailing & Final Dinner
itinerary_7_nights.description: A late afternoon private sunset sailing excursion around the headlands of Anguilla's northwest coast, with cocktails on the boat deck and the island's low profile lit amber in the low sun. Return for a final dinner at Malliouhana's signature restaurant, where the French-Caribbean tasting menu under the open-air terrace with the sea below is outstanding.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Clifftop Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: Last morning at the Malliouhana pools with the Caribbean stretching to the horizon. A final breakfast on the cliff terrace, one more swim in the sea below the hotel, and transfer to the airport or ferry dock.

caveats: Getting to Anguilla requires either a regional flight connection (via San Juan, Antigua, St. Kitts, or St. Martin) or the ferry from St. Martin/Sint Maarten. There are no direct international long-haul flights to Anguilla.
Anguilla is one of the most expensive Caribbean islands overall — dining and activities outside the hotel are premium-priced across the island, reflecting its upscale reputation.
Hurricane season (June–November) can affect Anguilla. The peak season is December through April; Malliouhana may close partially during the off-season — always verify opening dates when booking.

faqs.question: Is Malliouhana good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Excellent — particularly for couples who want exceptional architectural design, the Caribbean's most spectacular multi-level pool system, two private beaches below a dramatic headland, and Auberge Resorts' consistently outstanding service and cuisine. Anguilla itself is one of the most romantic Caribbean islands.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: December through April for the best weather — reliably sunny, warm (27–29°C), and low rainfall. February and March are the peak romantic season. May through November is hurricane season with lower rates but weather risk.

faqs.question: How far in advance should you book?
faqs.answer: Book 4–6 months ahead for peak season (January–March). The Headland Suite in particular books out very early. Ocean View Suites are usually available with 2–3 months' notice outside peak dates.

faqs.question: Is Malliouhana adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, families are welcome. However, the clifftop architecture (steep cliff access, pool system), fine dining focus, and premium pricing create a naturally adults-oriented atmosphere.

faqs.question: What is the best room type?
faqs.answer: The Headland Suite for the most dramatic clifftop position and panoramic views — a once-in-a-lifetime room. Ocean View Suites along the cliff edge for the full Malliouhana experience at a slightly lower price. All rooms benefit from the extraordinary multi-level pool access.

faqs.question: How do you get to Malliouhana?
faqs.answer: Fly into Clayton J. Lloyd International Airport (AXA) via connecting flight from San Juan (SJU), Miami, or other hubs; or fly to Princess Juliana Airport (SXM) in St. Martin/Sint Maarten and take the 20-minute passenger ferry to Anguilla. The hotel arranges all transfers from both the airport and the ferry dock.
